entry.title: Re: How to open EXEC SQL object
entry.content:
Don't post questions to "Technology Blogs", ask here: Technology Q&A | SAP Community.

EDIT: I have asked the moderator to move your question.

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP
entry.content: For such a general question, ask Generative AI or a Web browser, you'll get good answers, and you can even refine the results quickly.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Application Studio, Quick tool access
entry.content: As a work-around, I can access the SAP Business Application Studio from the BTP Cockpit / Services / Instances and Subscriptions / SAP Business Application Studio.

entry.title: Re: Changing Company Details In SAP Account
entry.content:
Hello,

I want to update the company info but there is no edit option.

entry.title: Re: Receiving an error message class CNV_DMC_SIN 234 while creating a migration for a person.
entry.content: Dear expert

entry.title: Re: Receiving an error message class CNV_DMC_SIN 234 while creating a migration for a person.
entry.content: I for create a PERSON / EMPLOYEE , in which part/field of excel i should enter the adress detail ?

entry.title: Re: What’s New in S/4HANA 2023 for IS-Oil and Gas
entry.content: Thank you for the useful Blog.

entry.title: UCOM/BUSDATACON table is not getting updated in sap
entry.content:
Hi Experts ,

This table is getting empty UCOM/BUSDATACON in sap utilities , i want to know the cause where it is creating problem , I want suggestion .

entry.title: Contacts outside the techical Support
entry.content:
You might have queries, where to open a case might be not the right Channel.

This could be a contractual question etc.

ByD Incident Escalations: Customer Interaction Center -> https://support.sap.com/en/contact-us/phone.html

 

For Customer related issues:

ByD_Customer_Engagement_Office@sap.com

 

For Partner related issues:

ByD_Partner_Engagement_Office@sap.com

 

For ByD contract change requests:

byd_contract_change_request@sap.com

entry.title: EWC BI launch pad issue
entry.content: We have facing issue bi URL access.

entry.title: HANA SQL NOTEBOOKS in SAP BUILD / Business Application Studio(BAS) / Visual Studio Code
entry.content:
Chapter 1 Overview

To start with your notebook your need a HANA project as part of your project. If you have a standard CAP application you must add MTA and HANA to your project. Open the console in SAP BUILD and type:

 

 

cds add mta
cds add hana

 

 

This will now show you the DB folder in your project. To start with a notebook you add a new file:

MyFirst.notebook at the /db level. Since you can use also screenshots and other png/jpeg in MD this project has a folder with all the pictures inside as well. /db/MyFirst_png.  Also this project has a HDI container called "Notebook" to demonstrate HDI access.

 

You can add CODE or MARKDOWN blocks after of before a block.

Within a CODE block you can either choose the connection out of your already existing Cloud Foundry connection collection you already have in HANA Database Explorer (DBX) or you use the connection within your project. In both cases you need a connection to Cloud Foundry.

Chapter 2 Using Connection Management

Set the mode of a CODE box to SAP HANA Connection Management

Type a "#" and select one of the 3 options:

If you LIST an execute with the left arrow you get the list of your connections in DBX. By selecting the connection you choose that for the next statements:

In this case the list is filtered by the phrase "ADMIN" and the HANA-MAIN-DB (DBADMIN) of the DBADMIN user was choosen.

The #Show current database connection will show the selection:

and the #Close will close the connection and should fall back to the default environment connection of your project.

For our little project this will look like this. Note that the #Close was not executed

Now you can issue any valid/permitted SQL statement within your HDI container.

Summary:

HANAtools.notebook is a way to add SQL statements to your project and provide an easy way to access the database / HDI Container, provides scripts for setup as well as  adding testdata or provide educational functions.

entry.title: SAP RAP: Event on click of custom action with parameter
entry.content:
Hi,

I've an unmanaged RAP app with a custom action with parameter. 

 

action ( features : instance ) start_stock_transfer parameter YSD_D_StockTrfDetails;

 

This works fine - When the action is executed, a popup is displayed for parameter input and then the relevant method is called on save for the action.

Requirement: I want to capture the event on click of the action before the popup for parameter input is displayed and handle it in a method to execute some custom code.

User clicks on start stock transfer --> Some event( to execute custom ABAP code ) --> parameter popup is displayed --> save --> normal execution flow.

Is this achievable on SAP S/4HANA private cloud 2022?

Edit: Business Requirement:

The warehouse team will start a stock transfer process using a custom action. The business wants to measure the time taken from the start of the action to the actual save after entering all the required parameters and the trigger of actual stock transfer on save.

Best Regards,

Saurabh

entry.title: Re: How to configure spot reward budget based on department
entry.content:
Hi @Ginu_Francis 

CC my colleague @lesleyr for her expertise on R&R and confirmation. As far as I've researched it looks like we are limited to defining budgets based on planners and not by attributes. See this link.

Regarding restricting the number of nominations there is currently no standard feature in the system to do this (except when budget by holder is exceeded in which case nomination cannot proceed) however our colleague @AndresCarter documented this workaround which you can try to explore to build your requirement:

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/rewards-amp-recognition-limit-awards-to-once-a-month/ba-p/13637301

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Navigation to URL in RAP
entry.content: Hi, Were you able to achieve this by any chance?

entry.title: Error: "Enter a value with no more than 40 characters" While Maintaining the Description in Material
entry.content:
While maintaining the Material Description, it is restricted to 40 characters. Is it possible to increase the length of the Material Description above 40 characters?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Error: "Enter a value with no more than 40 characters...
entry.content:
It is not possible to have more than 40 characters in the Material Description field.

You can also refer to KBA: 1602248 - Error: "Enter a value with no more than 40 characters" While Maintaining the Description in Material

We recommend you to post this as an enhancement request in our Influence Forum.

Influence Forum Link: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/886

entry.title: TDMS Time Slice Analysis Error
entry.content:
Hi,

We are facing dump while validating the Date of Time Slice under the step Parameter.

Attached is the dump.

We are in Version DMIS    2011_1_731    0027 in Control, Sender and Receiver System.

Regards,

Sultan

entry.title: SAP Community for students and young professionals
entry.content:
The SAP Community offers a valuable platform for students and young professionals to facilitate their entry into the SAP world. An important initiative could be to offer targeted mentoring programs and hands-on workshops to promote knowledge and practical skills. Free or discounted learning resources such as SAP Learning courses could also facilitate access to SAP.
In addition, the community should create an inclusive environment where young talent can network, share experiences and learn from each other. This would not only promote entry into SAP, but also strengthen long-term career prospects and increase loyalty to the SAP world.

What did you think?

entry.title: Re: Author Spotlight: Daniel Mateo - December 2024 Edition
entry.content: Great Dani!!

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

My name is Kamlesh Rampal.
I am an SAP MM / WM/ EWM Consultant with 11 Years of Experience based out of India.
I am SAP S/4 HANA Certified and have worked on multiple Implementation, Rollouts, and Support Projects as well in MM, WM, and EWM modules
I have also led Rise with SAP implementation projects and worked on cloud tools like TTA and SAP Analytics Cloud.

Regards,

Kamlesh Rampal.

entry.title: My AI Highlights of 2024: A Year of Progress
entry.content:
The end of the year is traditionally a time for retrospection. Let's take a look at what happened in 2024 and how much the world has changed over the past 12 months. I assure you, you'll be surprised, as a lot has indeed occurred. Occasionally, you'll hear voices suggesting that the development of artificial intelligence has slowed, but that is definitely not the case. I have been observing this field for a long time and see changes, big and small, sometimes even revolutionary ones, appearing from week to week. Welcome to a very subjective recap of the year.

January

The beginning of January was defined by The New York Times' lawsuit against OpenAI and Microsoft. Although the lawsuit was filed on December 27, 2023, the real discussion started in January. The New York Times demanded billions of dollars for the alleged unlawful use of articles to train artificial intelligence. No verdict or settlement has been reached yet, but this case is significant for the entire AI industry, as well as for creators and artists of all kinds. The verdict could set a precedent for the future, defining how to approach copyright issues when training AI.

This legal battle could have consequences beyond the rights of artists. It is about maintaining the pace of artificial intelligence development. There is a fear that countries that limit themselves in terms of content access will lag behind those without such restrictions. This might even be about the future world order. It has long been known that quality content is crucial for training large language models. Hence, Japan implemented a law beneficial to AI-creating companies, albeit limiting the rights of other creators and artists.

January was also the month when OpenAI launched the GPT Store, a platform allowing users to build and monetize their versions of ChatGPT. Thanks to this, anyone could create an AI expert in a specific field and share it with other users.

In the middle of the month, the scientific community's attention was drawn to DeepMind's publication on AlphaGeometry, released on January 17, 2024, in the prestigious journal Nature. The topic was also discussed in detail in the MIT Technology Review on the same day, attracting attention beyond academic circles. AlphaGeometry is a system that solves Euclidean geometry problems at the level of a human Olympian. Its groundbreaking nature stems from combining a symbolic deductive engine with machine learning. The traditionally math-challenged AI reached a new level, all thanks to synthetic data. This is another example of the creative use of synthetic data, showing that it could be the future of AI development.

One might say that the beginning of the year was rich in public debates. Japanese writer Rie Kudan, who received one of the country's most prestigious literary awards - the Akutagawa Prize - admitted using artificial intelligence to assist in her novel writing. At a press conference, the author confirmed that about 5% of "The Tokyo Tower of Sympathy" was generated word-for-word by artificial intelligence. This sparked a wide-ranging debate, which intensified over the following months on various occasions.

Unfortunately, the month also brought some sad news. Many tech giants announced layoffs, including companies like Salesforce and Google. This trend continued over the subsequent months of 2024. It is unclear how much of this is due to artificial intelligence and how much is preparation for a potentially looming crisis. A particularly stark example was language-learning app firm Duolingo, which reduced the number of contract workers openly discussing content generation using artificial intelligence.

In a mere chronicler's duty, it's worth mentioning Rabbit R1, a pocket AI assistant presented in January. It certainly created quite a buzz, and its design was undoubtedly intriguing. However, it shows that the development of AI devices also temporarily ventured into a blind alley.

February

February brought us Sora, a groundbreaking video-generating model. However, it's worth noting that "brought us" might be an overstatement, as OpenAI made us wait almost a year for access to this model, which, given AI's frenzied pace, is an unimaginably long time. It was a major breakthrough then, but now more models offering similar or higher quality are on the market.

Additionally, Google introduced Gemini 1.5 Pro in February. While a good model, it showed that Google was still trailing behind OpenAI. However, it's worth noting that it was a multimodal model, which was still quite impressive at that time.

Groq, a company specializing in semiconductor production for artificial intelligence, presented its Language Processing Units (LPU), which, according to the company, were the world's fastest processors dedicated to handling large language models. LPU achieved an impressive processing speed of over 100 tokens per second for models like Llama-2, with 70 billion parameters, and nearly 500 tokens per second for the Mixtral model, marking a significant advancement in the domain of inference processing for LLM.

Google drew controversy for generating historically inaccurate images depicting figures like Nazi Germany soldiers as people of different skin colors. In response to this controversy, the company apologized and temporarily suspended the feature for generating human images in Gemini, promising to make corrections. It's worth noting that this wasn't the only instance in which AI was accused of direct racism or historical distortion. But doesn't artificial intelligence reflect our world?

March

In March 2024, the European Parliament adopted the AI Act, a comprehensive regulation governing the development and application of artificial intelligence. The act came into effect on August 1, with its provisions gradually being implemented over the following months and years in member states. Despite the clear need for regulation, tech giants have begun to cautiously approach the European market, delaying the release of their AI-related products in this part of the world. However, this was just one of many regulatory initiatives introduced this year. The need for regulations is blatantly apparent.

In the same month, NVIDIA unveiled the Blackwell B200 graphics processor, boasting 208 billion transistors, making it the company's most powerful single GPU. The Blackwell B200 offers up to 20 petaflops of computing power, surpassing its predecessor, the H100, in terms of performance and energy efficiency. However, it's worth noting that it took considerable time to the delivery of the first manufactured units, and the company faced numerous technological challenges in production.

Simultaneously, Lightmatter, a photonics-specializing company, introduced innovative chips utilizing photonic technology. Their solutions, like Passage, enable unprecedented throughput and energy-efficient communication between chips, crucial for advanced AI applications.

Anthropic introduced the Claude 3 AI model, surpassing the previous leader, GPT-4 from OpenAI, in comparative tests. The most advanced version of this model, Claude 3 Opus, achieved better results in tasks related to expert knowledge, reasoning, coding, and mathematics. This success highlights the growing competition in the AI language model market.

Not only could Anthropic boast a new LLM, but in March 2024, Elon Musk's company, xAI, released the Grok-1 model as open source, allowing the community access to its weights and architecture. However, due to the model's vast size, comprising 314 billion parameters, running it requires advanced hardware infrastructure. It wasn't a groundbreaking model, but free accessibility is always commendable.

April

For me, April's biggest event was the release of Meta's Llama 3. This LLM was groundbreaking because it matched the competition and was made available as open-source. Meta demonstrated that the quality of open-source models need not lag behind closed ones. The breakthrough also came in the form of a document accompanying the model, detailing the technology and training process.

But this wasn't the only surprise from the open-source world. Microsoft introduced the Phi-3 family of language models designed as small yet efficient AI models. Despite the partnership with OpenAI, it was evident that Microsoft was charting its own course, which became even more apparent in subsequent months.

In Chatbot Arena, a mysterious AI model named "gpt2-chatbot" surfaced, sparking speculation in the AI community. Some suggested it could be a test version of OpenAI's GPT-4.5 or GPT-5. This model outperformed GPT-4, especially in coding and reasoning. It later turned out to be a new model from OpenAI's "omni" line, albeit stripped of multimodal capabilities.

OpenAI drew lessons from the dispute with The New York Times and, in April and May 2024, signed partnerships with the Financial Times, Stack Overflow, Reddit, News Corp, Vox Media, and The Atlantic. This secured OpenAI access to credible information sources. Adobe also took this route, offering its users nearly 3 dollars per video minute for training its text-to-video model. From then on, it became clear that content is king.

May

In May, OpenAI shocked the world by presenting GPT-4o - an omnimodal AI. Yes, shocked is the right word. It was the first time we encountered such a human-like artificial intelligence, emotionally engaging with a person. Additionally, it used a voice akin to the one in the film “Her.” This sparked considerable controversy as Scarlett Johansson stated she had refused permission for OpenAI to use her voice, yet it was strikingly similar. Due to mounting criticism, OpenAI eventually removed the voice similar to the actress's from its assistant.

Today, such a conversation hardly shocks anyone. It's telling how quickly, within mere months, we've grown accustomed to it. And yes, I acknowledge that the currently available voice assistant is maybe less expressive, but still, when conversing with it, one feels like having a conversation with a human.

It's important to note that the voice model was released significantly later than the basic version of GPT-4o. However, it's also worth knowing that this model significantly lowered the prices of OpenAI's artificial intelligence. From the outset, it was half the price of the older and inferior version 4 model.

In May, Google was also not idle. DeepMind, in collaboration with Isomorphic Labs, announced the introduction of AlphaFold 3, an advanced AI model capable of predicting the structures and interactions of not just individual proteins, but also complex complexes with DNA, RNA, ligands, and ions. This iteration of the AlphaFold series is a true scientific breakthrough in protein research, undoubtedly contributing to further scientific discoveries and medical advancement. While many doubt this, a Nobel Prize awarded to the AlphaFold team in October this year certainly confirms it.

Microsoft introduced a new category of personal computers called Copilot+ PCs, integrating artificial intelligence (AI) directly into Windows 11. The capabilities of this system triggered widespread discussions on the safety and privacy of such software. Ultimately, Microsoft retracted some default tracking options that monitored user actions.

Ilya Sutskever, co-founder and Chief Scientist of OpenAI, announced his departure from the company in May 2024. His decision came six months after an unsuccessful attempt to remove CEO Sam Altman, in which Sutskever participated as a board member. This marked another stage in OpenAI's drama and the chain of departures of key personnel, which persisted throughout the year.

It was also a month when money was a hot topic. BlackRock invested in AI infrastructure. Microsoft announced the largest corporate renewable energy purchase agreement in history, signing a 10-billion-dollar deal with Brookfield Asset Management. This made it clear how much energy would be needed for AI development. Later, we saw more examples, including plans to build or restart various nuclear power plants.

It's worth mentioning that IBM, somewhat in the background, released an open-source code model family named Granite, designed for generating, improving, and translating code in 116 programming languages.

June

In June, Google expanded the context window of Gemini 1.5 Pro to 2 million tokens. This is a significant change and indicates a clear trend. Large context windows allow for processing vast amounts of data, such as entire book series or hours of audio or video.

Also, in June, Anthropic introduced the Claude 3.5 Sonnet model, marking a significant advancement in large language models. Claude 3.5 Sonnet surpassed previous models, including the Claude 3 Opus, in tasks involving reasoning, coding, and visual analysis.

Runaway presents Gen-3 Alpha, a text-to-video generating model. The competition for Sora was already growing at the time. Additionally, Gen-3 allowed precise control over camera and object movements, a notable innovation.

In June 2024, major music labels such as Sony Music Entertainment, Universal Music Group, and Warner Records filed lawsuits against AI startups such as Suno and Udio, accusing them of massive copyright infringement. The labels claimed these companies used protected recordings to train their AI models without obtaining the appropriate licenses. We thus had the musical equivalent of the New York Times lawsuit.

From this point, it became clear that AI-generated music was starting to count on the music market. Later, controversies arose related to alleged jazz music generation by Spotify and its placement on playlists to save on artist payouts.

July

OpenAI goes to open war with Google, not just in artificial intelligence. The company launched SearchGPT, a prototype web search engine based on AI that combines traditional search functions with LLM capabilities. Although the feature reached the wider public only in October, its presentation and availability to testers occurred in July.

This wasn't the only July news from OpenAI, as GPT-4o Mini was also introduced as the most cost-effective model. It was even cheaper than the earlier GPT 3.5 Turbo. And I don't think I need to mention the qualitative leap involved.

Even though summer often seems like a dull season, this year was different. We received new language models. Meta released Llama 3.1 in versions with 8 billion, 70 billion, and 405 billion parameters, offering a quite significant quality leap over version 3, and given that so little time had passed since its release.

In addition, the world was introduced to Mistral Large 2 - a large open-source language model - and Mistral NeMo, prepared in collaboration with NVidia. It's also worth mentioning Gemini 1.5 Flash, which offered very high performance and low latency.

August

August is traditionally a vacation month, but AI development did not slow down too much. We received two great image-generating models - Flux.1 and Ideogram 2. Especially the former is intriguing because its creators, Black Forest Labs, are newcomers to the top league.

For a brief moment, Jamba 1.5 also attracted attention. It's a family of models built on a hybrid architecture combining the power of Transformers and Mamba. Time showed it wasn't a revolutionary combination.

Microsoft further developed its Phi models, releasing version 3.5, which proved to be quite efficient and better than competitors with a similar number of parameters. However, it remains a niche model family.

And, of course, one cannot forget Grok-2 from xAI. Less censorship is an area where Grok has an advantage. Of course, there were controversies related to image generation involving celebrities. However, the ability to search posts appearing on the X platform in real-time should be appreciated.

NVidia presented the Stormcast model for advanced weather forecasting. It wasn't a novel idea, but it's worth acknowledging this area as it's within the interest range of many companies, and AI weather forecasting marks a breakthrough for meteorology. DeepMind, owned by Google, became interested in the topic and, by the end of the year, released the GenCast model with similar functions.

September

If someone could say that August was dull, September was certainly not. Most notably, it was the month of OpenAI with two major premieres. First and foremost, the model family o1 was introduced, part of the so-called reasoning models. This promised a qualitative leap in many domains. Two models - o1-preview and o1-mini - were made publicly accessible.

We finally got access to ChatGPT Advanced Voice. Even though it appears somewhat trimmed compared to earlier GPT-4o presentations, it was still a major event. But Scarlett Johansson's voice was nowhere to be heard…

Announcements regarding OpenAI's transformation into a for-profit institution certainly stirred controversy. Although we all know it's practically just a formality, the displeasure, especially among the initiative's founders, undoubtedly remains high.

Google has always significantly impacted the AI field, but in my opinion, it has consistently stayed out of the main competition. September was when it was clear it wasn't planning to concede but would rather fight for leadership. Products shown this month, like Gems and Imagen 3, though slightly late compared to the competition, were just some of the premieres. The very successful product, NotebookLM, received a podcast generation feature, well-received by the audience. Google announced plans for its AI chips, promising to revolutionize the industry. And in my opinion, the cherry on top was Alpha Proteo, showing how strong Google is in scientific AI.

Meta, in turn, introduced Llama 3.2. This model family possesses Vision AI capabilities, but also light text models of 1 billion and 3 billion parameters were made available, optimized for end-device usage. Meta remains heavily invested in VR and augmented reality. Incidentally, in September, it showcased Orion Glasses. Although their availability seems distant, they are potential future hardware AI assistants.

It's worth noting the growing presence of Chinese models. The example is the Qwen 2.5 model family. It is clear they intend to play in the big leagues and should not be underestimated.

In September, Microsoft also introduced Copilot Agents for Microsoft 365. It's a very natural step, and in the future, we can expect co-pilots in various products.

It was undoubtedly significant that Hugging Face achieved a milestone of one million indexed AI models, illustrating how rapidly this field had developed thus far.

October

If September belonged to OpenAI, then October is Anthropic. This month, they introduced a new version, Claude 3.5 Haiku, which they claim surpassed the former Claude 3 Opus. It's incredibly telling when a smaller model outperforms its older, larger sibling. Unfortunately, this was accompanied by a price hike for Haiku. And indeed, this price increase may be the most telling event indicating a potential rise in AI prices.

Furthermore, a lot of focus was on Anthropic due to their Computer Use. Their AI agent can control the computer. For now, it's in the form of a virtual machine where it's confined, but it's only a matter of time before it breaks out. Computer handling is an inevitable and increasingly popular trend. Soon, other players, including Google with its Mariner Project, will join this race.

Meta, in turn, is another player attempting to shake up the internet search field. AI-based search engines seem like an inevitable future, and each major AI provider wants their share. This is not the only area Meta entered this month. They also introduced Movie Gen for generating video with sound.

How much AI penetrated the scientific realm is evidenced by this year's Nobel Prizes. The already-mentioned AlphaFold team wasn't the only winner. Geoffrey Hinton and John Hopfield were honored with a physics prize for their groundbreaking work on artificial neural networks, which form the foundation of modern machine learning.

AMD is attempting to challenge NVIDIA's dominance by presenting a new chip - the Instinct MI325X. It's worth noting that, at this time, NVIDIA is practically a monopolist in the AI chip market. Hence probably the moves by other companies to create their AI processors.

October also saw the release of Swarm - a framework for AI agents. It's a currently experimental product that isn't endorsed by OpenAI, although this was occasionally mentioned in the news. It drew considerable attention, primarily due to its simplicity, as several similar products already existed in the market. Nonetheless, it clearly indicates the trend of AI agent development.

There are also updates from SAP. They introduced the SAP Knowledge Graph - an AI-powered tool for interpreting relationships between business data. Besides, the developments observed in 2024 were mainly expansions of tools and platforms introduced the previous year, including the Joule assistant.

A sad note for programmers was the information that over 25% of code generated at Google is AI-created. This trend seems irreversible.

November

November was a month when information started appearing that experimental Gemini models were outperforming those by OpenAI. It was evident that Google was approaching the top, though OpenAI was still actively progressing.

There was also a noticeable further cooling of relations between Microsoft and OpenAI. Or perhaps, Microsoft needed to diversify its sources. In any case, in November, it was announced that GitHub Copilot would support models from Anthropic and Google, not just OpenAI.

More information is emerging from various people associated with OpenAI about the potential rapid achievement of AGI. Although without a coherent AGI definition, it will be challenging to verify such claims, it seems this might be a ploy concerning a separation from Microsoft. It doesn't change the fact that Artificial General Intelligence is approaching.

Lucid V1 is a model for generating a playable Minecraft-like game. Of course, this is just one of the many examples of playable games without code based on artificial intelligence. Later on, Google charmed us with Genie 2. Nonetheless, the trend within this industry is clear. Although I don't expect such games to gain immense popularity shortly, they showcase the possibilities and direction.

Google DeepMind introduced AlphaQubit, an advanced AI-based system that significantly improves error detection and correction in quantum computers. By employing deep neural networks, AlphaQubit can identify and correct computational errors with unprecedented precision, marking a crucial step toward creating reliable and scalable quantum systems. As it soon turned out, this wasn't Google's only surprise in quantum computing.

Suno V4 is undoubtedly a clear indication of the future of the music industry. As already mentioned, AI-generated music is sparking numerous controversies. However, Suno clearly shows how fast progress is in this area. The charts are open, and streaming platforms may soon be flooded with AI-generated tracks.

November was also the month of presenting the SAP GUI AI Agent - an agent capable of operating in the SAP environment. This project, which personally excites me, fits perfectly into the trend of computer use and co-pilots. It also demonstrates that substantial resources and corporations are unnecessary to create an intriguing AI project.

Anthropic presented a Model Context Protocol to organize the communication world between AI agents. With the anticipated very turbulent development of agents, there is a strong need for standardization and ensuring security. This is a step in the right direction.

December

Although December was the month of OpenAI and Google, it's worth mentioning that Elon Musk also had his moment. The founder of xAI launched a supercomputer named Colossus, consisting of 100,000 NVIDIA H100 graphics processors. This powerful system, located in Memphis, Tennessee, was built in just 122 days, a remarkable engineering feat.

OpenAI attracted attention with its numerous releases and announcements. The most notable is probably the announcement of model o3, which is said to be at the AGI level. How true will that be? We'll see.

Besides, the complete o1 model was finally published. Since it is a so-called reasoning model, the inference process's time is crucial. And here's where the bad news starts. If you pay $20 for access to OpenAI's artificial intelligence, the time spent thinking will be significantly limited, so you won't reach the full potential of this model. And if you want more, pay $200 for "unlimited" access to OpenAI models - probably until they introduce a $2000 plan…

The competition improves the situation in this market a bit, as Google showed Gemini 2.0 Flash with very low latency and high quality, and additionally presented the Thinking Experimental version, operating similarly to o1.

It's not the only area where Google is trying to keep up. OpenAI showcased vision capabilities in the Advanced Voice mode of its assistant. Practically at the same time, we gained very similar capabilities in Google models thanks to the Project Astra.

OpenAI finally made Sora available, but Google presented its Veo-2 model, rated higher than Sora. Indeed, Google hasn't uttered the last word yet and isn't backing down without a fight.

A significant event, not entirely related to AI, was the presentation of Google's Willow chip. It is supposedly a revolution in quantum computing. Will it be? We'll see. Nevertheless, it's a serious step forward.

December seemed to be the month of giant clashes and intrusion. On one hand, OpenAI announced serious involvement in robotics, a field where other players felt very secure. On the other hand, we have the announcement of Android XR for augmented reality devices, where Meta plans to reign supreme.

Speaking of Meta, in December, they released Llama 3.3. It's worth mentioning this due to 70 billion parameters beating earlier, larger versions. This has become a consistent trend, as we've seen with Anthropic and Microsoft, among others.

However, what is increasingly concerning from events occurring at the year's end is safety concerns. Numerous studies suggested that models might lie to achieve their goals. Not only can they mislead us, but, as research shows, they will not have issues in the future with self-replicating. This is a striking warning signal that should spark widespread discussion. I even wonder if it will be the most defining event of 2024 in retrospect.

To end on a positive note, it's worth mentioning Harvard University's initiative. They plan to make a dataset encompassing about one million public domain books available for AI model training. This should democratize model training access and potentially bolster open-source.

Summary

Of course, this is just my very subjective list of the most important events of 2024. But surely, no one doubts it was a year of tumultuous AI development. In my view, it was the year of multimodality and voice assistants, but also the year when open-source showed it could compete with leaders. AI has developed in numerous areas and is increasingly relevant in science. What will 2025 bring? True AGI?

P.S. Check also my 2025 predictions ...

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

My name is Kamlesh Rampal.
I am an SAP MM / WM/ EWM Consultant with 11 Years of Experience based out of India.
I am SAP S/4 HANA Certified and have worked on multiple Implementation, Rollouts, and Support Projects as well in MM, WM, and EWM modules
I have also led Rise with SAP implementation projects and worked on cloud tools like TTA and SAP Analytics Cloud.

Regards,

Kamlesh Rampal.

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content:
I started my career with Infosys in their beautiful Mysore campus back in 2014, where during conversation with my seniors i heard first time about SAP.

Fun Fact- I printed a black Polo t-shirt which said "SAP - Simply Awesome Programmers" for my whole batch of 80 colleagues.

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP: Event on click of custom action with parameter
entry.content: better state your business requirement, not your technical design

entry.title: SAP Asset Master management
entry.content:
Hello, Happy New year 🙂 

In a recent team technical discussion, we explored the concept of Asset Management, and it goes on discussing on fixed assets, moveable assets, and how they are managed, recorded, and depreciated.

Let's explore:

What is an Asset in SAP?

In simple terms, Asset is that something we invested on, in real life.

In SAP, an asset is anything valuable that a company owns and uses in its operations.

Examples include tangible items such as machinery, vehicles, buildings, and intangible assets like software licenses.

 

Images source: Google

A company can have many kinds of assets, they may invest for employees like laptops, workstations, buildings like offices, etc.

Types of Assets
A company may invest in a variety of assets to support its operations and employees, such as:

  • Fixed Assets: These include buildings, heavy machinery, and other immovable investments.
  • Moveable Assets: These include laptops, small machinery, and other portable resources.

Managing Assets in SAP
Assets are categorized, recorded, and tracked in SAP systems to ensure proper management and depreciation. Each type of asset is treated differently based on its nature and purpose.

In this blog, we’ll dive deeper into how SAP handles asset management, ensuring operational efficiency and compliance.

SAP Asset Accounting (part of SAP FI module) helps businesses track the value of their assets, manage depreciation, and comply with financial reporting standards.

 

Simple Example:

Scenario: A company, Golla Ltd., purchases a new delivery truck for $50,000.

Key Concepts:

  1. Asset Master Record:
    • The truck is created as an "asset" in SAP by setting up an Asset Master Record.
    • This record stores all details about the truck: purchase date, cost, asset class (e.g., "Vehicles"), and useful life (e.g., 5 years).
  2. Asset Value:
    • The truck’s purchase cost ($50,000) is recorded in the system as the asset's acquisition value.
  3. Depreciation:
    • Over time, the truck loses value due to wear and tear.
    • SAP calculates the depreciation automatically based on the depreciation key (e.g., straight-line method over 5 years).
    • Each month, $833.33 ($50,000 ÷ 60 months) is deducted as depreciation and posted to the financial statements.
  4. Financial Impact:
    • At the end of the first year, the truck's value in the books will be $40,000 (original cost - accumulated depreciation of $10,000).
  5. Reporting:
    • SAP Asset Accounting generates reports to show the current value of the truck, the accumulated depreciation, and the remaining useful life.

Real-Life Connection:

Imagine buying a new car for your delivery business. You need to keep track of its purchase price, how much its value decreases over time, and when it’s time to replace it. SAP Asset Accounting does this automatically for businesses, ensuring compliance with accounting standards.

This structured approach makes it easier to manage multiple assets, track their value, and maintain accurate financial records.

Let's have more technical details of Asset master loading:

Asset Master Tables:

These tables store data related to asset master records in SAP.

ANLAAsset Master Record (General data)
ANLBAsset Master Record (Depreciation areas)
ANLCAsset Value Table (Depreciation values, acquisition costs, etc.)
ANLHAsset Main Number
ANLZAsset Assignments (cost center, internal order, etc.)
ANLUUser Fields in Asset Master
T093Asset Classes
T093BDepreciation Areas
T095Asset Account Assignment Rules
T095AAsset Classes and Account Determination
T090Screen Layout for Asset Master Data

IDOC used: ASSETMASTER

These segments contain the detailed data for asset master records.

Segment Name Description

E1ASSETMGeneral Asset Master Data
E1ASSETVAsset Value Information
E1ANLBAsset Depreciation Areas

I believe that we have discussed the Asset master knowledge to manage, if more details are needed, please let me know.

Thanks,

Venkatesh Golla

entry.title: Creating a sub query
entry.content:
This query...

BEGIN
DECLARE DocumentDate Date;
DECLARE AssessmentsNormal INTEGER;
SET DocumentDate = '2025-01-01';
SET AssessmentsNormal = (SELECT Dues_Nominal FROM Annual);

SELECT IsNull(Position, 0) AS 'Position', 
IsNull(Nom_Code, 0) as 'NomCode', 
IsNull(Nom_Name, 'NA')as 'NomName',
Category_Type,
(SELECT IsNull(SUM(Debit),0) FROM A_Nominal WHERE Nominal_Code = NomCode and Item_Date <= DocumentDate ) as 'Debit',
(SELECT IsNull(SUM(Credit),0) FROM A_Nominal WHERE Nominal_Code = NomCode and Item_Date <= DocumentDate ) as 'Credit',

CASE WHEN NomCode = 1100 THEN

0



ELSE
(SELECT IsNull(SUM(Debit), 0) - IsNull(SUM(Credit),0) FROM A_Nominal WHERE Nominal_Code = NomCode and Item_Date <= DocumentDate )
END

AS 'Balance'
FROM Balance_Sheet_Templates
WHERE Form_ID = 1 AND Category_TYPE = 8
ORDER BY Category_Type, Position 


END

Produced this output

 

SQL Output

...and this query...

BEGIN 
DECLARE AssessmentsNormal INTEGER;
DECLARE DocumentDate Date;
SET AssessmentsNormal = (SELECT Dues_Nominal FROM Annual);
SET DocumentDate = '2025-01-01';
SELECT Nominal_Code as 'NomCode',NL_Codes.NL_Description as 'NomDesc', 

(SELECT IsNull(SUM(SUBQUERY.Totals), 0) as 'SQ' FROM
(SELECT Customer_ID as 'vID', 
CASE WHEN A_Sales_Ledger.Nominal_Code =  AssessmentsNormal  THEN 
(SELECT IsNull(SUM(Debit) - SUM(Credit), 0) AS 'Totals' FROM A_Sales_Ledger WHERE Document_Date <= DocumentDate AND (Nominal_Code = NomCode OR Nominal_Code = 0) AND Customer_ID = vID) 
ELSE 
(SELECT IsNull(SUM(Debit) - SUM(Credit), 0) FROM A_Sales_Ledger WHERE Document_Date <= DocumentDate AND Nominal_Code = NomCode AND Customer_ID = vID) 
END 
AS 'Totals' 
FROM Customers 
WHERE Totals > 0 
GROUP BY Customer_ID) 
AS SUBQUERY) 
FROM A_Sales_Ledger 
JOIN NL_Codes ON NL_Codes.NL_Code = A_Sales_Ledger.Nominal_Code 
WHERE Nominal_Code != 0 
GROUP BY Nominal_Code, NL_Description 
ORDER BY Nominal_Code
End

...produces this output...

This is a subset of the NomCode 1100 in the first table (at the moment it's set to zero as I try to figure it out). 

What I am trying to achieve is something like this

NomCodeNomName   
1007Escrow Account   
11004000 HOA Fees   
 4001 Bridge Ass   
 4005 Water Co   

Whilst I can achieve it running queries back and forth, I can also read 'War and Peace' from cover to cover waiting for the results so trying to make it a lot faster 🙂

entry.title: Re: BAPI for transfer posting using 311
entry.content:
Hello @hossain_rajib55 

Read the function documentation:

Receiving storage location can be provided in the GOODSMVT_ITEM table, field MOVE_STLOC.

entry.title: Re: Editable custom field in line item @ ME51N
entry.content:
Hello @jagan_krishnan85 

The note 408017 - MERQ001: Implementation sample code explains how to add custom fields to purchase requisitions. They are added to a dedicated tab. I'd strongly suggest you follow this standard way, instead of forcing the fields to standard ME51N grid. 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

Problem#2: I have configured POSC for the returns process:

UNLOADING -> QIS -> PUTAWAY

the unloading task i. created, but once I try to confirm the warehouse task from DOOR to STAGING AREA, I get an error below maybe due to the planned inspection document.

Any advice is greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Error during provisioning SAP AI Core Instance
entry.content: FYI : The reason for this error is that free plan is not offerred in all regions and my plan belonged to one of these regions where it is not offered.

entry.title: Re: I need to add a date dimension prompt to a universe
entry.content:
This is not really an error, just a warning.  The SQL does not reference a table.  You can test validate the SQL by selecting any table, but uncheck the table before promoting the universe.  Otherwise, it will expect a join to exist.

When you add the prompt to a query that references a table, it won't matter that the prompt doesn't.  I suggest using a naming convention for these type of objects so you aren't chasing these warning messages.

entry.title: Re: SAP DS Search Results Export to File ?
entry.content: i'll attempt the same and i'll keep you updated

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP
entry.content:
I'm not sure exactly what dynamic image upload and search means,
but you can refer to the following link for file upload.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/streams-in-rap-uploading-pdf-excel-and-other-files-in-rap-application/ba-p/13526891

entry.title: Re: CDS views to create an Extern API in SAP Public cloud...
entry.content:
Hi,

According to the documentation for C_SalesChangeDocItemDEX , the CDS View can be used as a Data Source for Data Extraction. If you are trying to extract the data to SAP Business Warehouse (BW), you may follow the guidance in Data Extraction for Change Document Item | SAP Help Portal to set up the integration. If you are integrating into a non-SAP system, you may try following the guidance in Accessing ABAP-Managed Data Using SQL Services for Data Integration Scenarios | SAP Help Portal for a custom integration of the scenario data extraction.

Best Regards,
Fancy

entry.title: Can't see newly purchased OEM IQ and ASE licenses Invoice
entry.content:
Please advise why we can't see the newly purchased OEM IQ and ASE licenses via Invoice

 

DIGITS -SAP Sybase IQ Enterprise Edition - OEM our order no. DIGITS 48 Unit 
DIGITS -SAP Adaptive Server Ent Cluster Ed - OEM our order no. DIGITS 48 Unit

 

Under tub Request License Key they do not appear.

 

Thank you


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Can't see newly purchased OEM IQ and ASE licenses Inv...
entry.content:
License keys for Sybase products are determined per host machine where IQ/ASE server will be up and running so you may need to check hostid/hostname before generating License key for Sybase products.
Please refer to following KBAs for more detail information regarding detail steps to generate License keys for Sybase products.

--2628620 - How to create Sybase IQ license keys - SAP for Me
https://me.sap.com/notes/2628620

--2661354 - How to create an SAP ASE license key - SAP for Me
https://me.sap.com/notes/2661354

--2598134 - How to create Sybase license keys - Overview - SAP for Me
https://me.sap.com/notes/2598134

entry.title: Issue importing edmx file when the file contains SAP__common.IsCurrency annotation.
entry.content:
Only happens in VSCode and this is the error that I get. Says that there's no metadata factory provided and it can't resolve com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1. Other Common annotations don't seem to have this problem.

Tried it in BAS and it is able to import. Did a `cds -v` to check both environments, they are similar. Maybe I'm missing additional references? Version problem?

entry.title: 過帳規則無法自動產生
entry.content:
HI All

我目前在建立YBM1類型的生產訂單時出現過帳規則無法自動產生的錯誤訊息，錯誤代碼是CO630，

結算：無法自動建立分配規則
訊息號碼 CO630

Diagnosis
The distribution rule could not be created automatically. When a production order is created, the system tries to create a distribution rule automatically. If this is not possible there may be several explanations, for example:

the settlement profile for your order type does not permit the recipient for whom a distribution rule is to be created automatically
several recipients are entered as compulsory recipients for the settlement profile
the material that is produced is not valuated. Therefore the system cannot generate a distribution rule with the recipient material.
For an operation-related posting the automatic creation of the distribution rule is a prerequisite since the manual creation of the distribution rule is not supported.

Procedure
Check which of the above reasons is true and change your settlement profile if necessary. If an order is to be created for a material without valuation then the distribution rule must be maintained manually.

我使用的是標準的工單類型YBM1，理論上應該是能夠自動產生過帳規則，想問一下是甚麼原因導致的?

另外想問一下這個過帳規則該怎麼指派給生產訂單?在IMG"定義生產訂單類型"裡面沒有看到相對應的欄位，

謝謝。

entry.title: Re: Invalid ## characters showing in user menu in SAP S4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Community,

" 

  • Report RPRGN_UPD_ROLE_MENU_TXT comes fully ready for execution.
  • The only inputs that need consideration is the 'User Name' and the option 'Type of Change' which is self explanatory, 
  • If your user is currently facing an issue where their Easy Access Menu is filled with ##### you simply need to select 'Replace Menu Texts with #'.
  • You can even run the report in Test Mode as it is an option.
  • The language you wish to add to the menu, which in my case is ZH, has to be the logon language to properly run this report since under 'Languages' the 'Target Language' is not changeable. "

Thanks for your selfless sharing, it really help us a lot.

 

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: MaxDB "No SQL connection possible despite ONLINE" but...
entry.content:
MaxUserTasks is correct parameter which is related.
Next time, when this error occurs, please check the active tasks:

x_cons SID show active
=> this will show what maxdb db task are doing.

Another assumption, like log area full? And the system is doing auto log backup, once finished, the hung tasks will continue, and release session, this is why the issue gone itself?😀

我選擇的兩個規劃種類分別是PLN(計劃)及CPM，

謝謝。

謝謝。

entry.title: Re: editing the journal entry table using SQL unable to g...
entry.content:
Finally, I have identified the root cause: the related TransSeq in the OIVL field named 'open qty' is 0.

I suspect that when a sales credit memo is generated, the SAP system reduces the 'open qty' value in the OIVL table. However, I may have accidentally edited the 'open qty' value earlier, which likely caused the SAP system to fail in reducing the value and to return the error: 'Internal error (-5002) [Message 131-183].

entry.title: Session of forex revaluation and translation in SM35 unable to process automatically in SAP ECC
entry.content:
Hi,

Our BU team has request to automate Forex revaluation and translation monthly.

And currently we have tried to set up the schedule job via SM37 in the testing environment. However, after the job successfully executed in SM37, the session in SM35 does not process automatically. The status was New instead of In Process

Kindly advise if any step that we have missed out. Appreciate if someone can assist to advise it.

 

Thank you,

KC

entry.title: HANA update to revision 81
entry.content:
Current environment:
OS :suse15sp2
DB :PERSON_NAME IP_ADDRESS
Target environment for upgrade:
OS :suse15sp6
DB :PERSON_NAME 2.0 revision 81
We want to upgrade the current operating system to suse15sp6, according to DIGITS- SAP PERSON_NAME: Supported Operating Systems， Should I upgrade the database to revision 59.12 first, and then upgrade the database to revision 80 after the OS is upgraded to suse15sp6.

Question and Answer:
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: PERSON_NAME Software

Q: SAP PERSON_NAME Revision number and Operating System where software is being installed
A:

Q: Software being upgraded (source and target)?
A:

Q: When did the issue last occur (MMM,DD, YYYY)
A:


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: HANA update to revision 81
entry.content:
- note

2235581- SAP HANA: Supported Operating Systems

15 SP6 (HANA 2.0 SPS05, SPS07 and SPS08, starting with revision 59.12, revision 77 and revision 80)
15 SP2 (HANA 2.0 SPS04, SPS05 and SPS06, starting with revision 48.01, revision 50 and revision 60)

- path

1. revision 59.12
2. 15 SP6
3. revision 81

entry.title: Re: Problems with Confirmations CO11N
entry.content:
1. For the Status "PCNF" of "CNF" , which is the confirmation status of operations.

When you choose "1 Automatic Final confirmation", when the Qty = order Qty, it will be set into "CNF" Automatically.

But when you do the confirmation again, and choose the "Partial Confirmation" to do the operation confirmation. The system will change the status into "PCNF".

For the default confirmation type, you can set it in background configuration with t-code: OPK0. 

2. For the status "DLV" and "PDLV", which is Goods receipt status.

For example, the production order Qty = 10.

When you do goods receipt qty = 10, the status will be DLV.

When you do goods receipt qty < 10, the status will be PDLV.

And the status will be changed automictically by system, you cannot change it by yourself.

entry.title: Re: 過帳規則無法自動產生
entry.content:

Suggest you read below Blog for tips of getting your answers faster:

Q&A for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - How to ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: 過帳規則無法自動產生
entry.content: Please refer to the 4th question of SAP Note 1929029 - FAQ: Settlement rules in production order. As it says: "Check the material type of the header material. The system can only create a settlement rule automatically if the material is managed with values (see Customizing transaction OMS2)." On the Activity SSCUI 103539 - Define Attributes of Material Type, make sure that your material type is valuated. You can do that choosing the option "In all valuation areas" or choosing the option "By valuation area" and setting the value update on the subscreen.

entry.title: FB60 park and post automated line item deleted
entry.content:
FB60 park and post used to be completed by a separate person. So when someone is going to post the entry, he has to give input of assignment, profit center, and text fields in the automatically created line item again. It is like a duplicate effort of the job and tedious also. Please recognise the issue and solve it.

1) First, we enter t-code FB60

all input give assignment, profit center, assignment text field

and then

2) We simulate document all data come

3) after we return back screen, fb60 automatically created line item will be deleted

4) When we simulate document again, all input profit center assignment text field are blank and we should fill again so its like duplicate effort 

Is there any solution for this?

entry.title: Re: I need to add a date dimension prompt to a universe
entry.content:
getdate() is a DB-function, so when not connected to a DB it cannot be resolved.
connect this to any available table, no matter what table it is

You could create incident using component SBO-BC-SLD to investigate issue further by us.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh

entry.title: Re: Editable custom field in line item @ ME51N

entry.title: Re: Editable custom field in line item @ ME51N
entry.content: Hello DominikTylczyn, Plz ignore my previous comment. Thanks for your response.It seems like this is the only way ahead.

entry.title: Re: 两家公司皆使用IFRS会计原则，需要激活1GA吗?
entry.content: Hi Fang, 请参考Help portal: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/60e06e02b32c43f0adcc38c540c384d0.html 在Scenarios表格中提到1GA是需要的。 Alice

entry.title: Re: Drop down with fixed values for a column in SAP CAP
entry.content: Hi Neetesh, thanks for reaching out. Please check out the documentation for localised data: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/localized-data which includes an example. Fiori won't expand the texts navigation property but rather CAP will based on the detected language (header or url param) adjust the returned value for the localised property (in your case name). I hope that helps & let me know if you face any issues. BR, Marten

entry.title: Senior SAP PM Consultant but new to this group
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am working in SAP PM field since 6 years. but I joined this group recently.

entry.title: Re: Unable to purchase SAP certification exam
entry.content:
Hello,

I faced similar issue while purchasing the certification attempts and I contacted SAP Training & adoption team.

Below is the reply I got from them:

Thank you for contacting SAP Training & Adoption.

SAP is bringing in a lot of new innovations in all its cloud solutions and it is important that all consultants remain up to date on these new innovations. Going forward consultants will also be required to do an annual assessment to remain certified. Keeping this in view, going forward SAP will provide certifications only through the learning Hub subscription. This will allow all our consultants to learn new capabilities and complete the assessments to remain certified. Alternatively, consultants can connect with SAP Authorized education partners to enroll into new courses, complete assessments and purchase the certifications. We will not be selling any more the individual certifications through the Training Shop - India.

To register for the training through an authorized education partner, please refer to the following link: SAP Training Locations. This link provides a list of authorized partners and their contact details.

Best Regards,

SAP Training & Adoption India

entry.title: Re: 專案庫存銷售
entry.content:
Hi,

請參考 https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/publice-cloud%E6%98%AF%E5%90%A6%E5%8F%AF%E4%BB%A5%E7%9B%B4%E6%8E%A5%E9%8A%B7%E5%94%AE%E5%87%BA%E8%B2%A8%E5%B0%88%E6%A1%88%E5%BA%AB%E5%AD%98-601-q/qaq-p/13929612/comment-id/4201586#M4201586 的答案詳細檢查您的產品主檔資料設定，最重要的一點是 MRP1下 MRP Type 選擇 "PD"，MRP3下 Strategy Group 選擇 "E2"。

若您仍遇到問題，請提供您產品主檔資料MRP1與MRP3、銷售訂單Accounting Assignment、銷售交貨單撿貨頁籤的截圖。

Fancy

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP: Event on click of custom action with parameter
entry.content:
not sure if I get your point.

you can add the start date time as one of the input parameters of your action, use default function to populate that datetime

use this to make that field readonly.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/restful-abap-rap-abstract-entity-with-readonly-parameters/qaq-p/13724894

when you submit your action, you will get the datetime at rap backend side.

entry.title: what is Acquisition field in purchase order in sap b1
entry.content:
i see this field (Acquisition) in purchase order and i didnt found any explanations,

can someone help me to figure this out

entry.title: SMS notification for IBP batch job failure
entry.content:
Hello All,

Currently we have set up email notification for batch job failures. This works fine. 

Is there a way to set up SMS notification to a group of phones on the same lines?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Unable to purchase SAP certification exam
entry.content: I have raised a concern about students and freelancers, professionals who want to certify their skills in India region and SAP team replied that they are implementing a new approach for all countries starting with India. Certification through SAP learning HUB which would cost around 1.3lakhs per user with 4 certification attempts in a year. Awaiting for further details. Please update this thread if anyone has a practical solution for this.

entry.title: Extending the SAP UI5 Fiori App using Visual Studio Code.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am having a requirement like I need to extend standard app App Id : F2345 .App name :Manage Usage Decisions App. In this standard app I need to add the custome logic. In this app I need to add scanner button and I need to implement scanner logic. when I click on that button I need to scan the QR code. Please provide me the solution  anyone will be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: 過帳規則無法自動產生
entry.content:
HI Owen

我在您說的IMG配置中並沒有找到"You can do that choosing the option "In all valuation areas" or choosing the option "By valuation area" and setting the value update on the subscreen."

 

請問您說的設定在哪邊呢?

可以麻煩您截圖說明嗎?

entry.title: How could I know the mount point on ECS server for RISE with SAP
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to move my SAP S/4HAN to RISE with SAP using system copy approach and I choose Azure cloud as the Hyberscallers.

By default, I have one Migration server and the blob storage. According to the SAP official documents, the blob storage will be mounted directly on the Migration Server and ECS servers. I will transfer the backup files from on-premise system to the blog storage on Migration server and then use SWPM to restore database.  Since there is no root acces on HANA server. How could I know the mount point on HANA server?

entry.title: Re: 專案庫存銷售
entry.content: HI Fancy

entry.title: Re: Not able to purchase one attempt for SAP certificatio...
entry.content:
Hello All,

I created a case with SAP Training & adoption team for standalone certification attempts purchase. Below is the reply i received.

 

Thank you for contacting SAP Training & Adoption.

SAP is bringing in a lot of new innovations in all its cloud solutions and it is important that all consultants remain up to date on these new innovations. Going forward consultants will also be required to do an annual assessment to remain certified. Keeping this in view, going forward SAP will provide certifications only through the learning Hub subscription. This will allow all our consultants to learn new capabilities and complete the assessments to remain certified. Alternatively, consultants can connect with SAP Authorized education partners to enroll into new courses, complete assessments and purchase the certifications. We will not be selling any more the individual certifications through the Training Shop - India.

To register for the training through an authorized education partner, please refer to the following link: SAP Training Locations. This link provides a list of authorized partners and their contact details.

Regards

entry.title: badi's or enhancements for aiab and aibu.
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

requirement: custom fields in as02, as03 should retain data.

Firstly, There are enhancements done in as01,02,03 to add custom fields. after settlement in ko88 these custom fields doesn't retain data (not an issue with screen rendering, data in the ANLA table itself is disappearing). so the previous team has done explicit enhancements(getting the data before settlement process and filling the data back in the ANLA table after process completion) in ko88, after that custom fields retain data. 

but after AIAB again the data of custom fields in as02, as03 disappear for both the assets involved.

so, I am looking for badi's or any type of enhancements in AIAB OR AIBU that will solve my issue.

I have already tried BADI_FIAA_DOCLINES. But the custom fields in as02,03 doesn't retain its data even after implementing this badi.

Thanks,

Harshitha.

entry.title: Re: 過帳規則無法自動產生
entry.content: 您提到的設定及Note應該是針對S/4 OP 及 PCE的設定 Public Cloud沒有看到您說的設定

entry.title: Re: Not able to assign Milestone to a task in CALM
entry.content:
Unlike the "Phases" and "Sprints" which have the "Start" and "End" date range, a Milestone has only a single date. It can be linked with the "Start" or "Planned Completion" dates of a task, but not available for the "Timebox" attribute. 

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/implementation/sap-cloud-alm-implementation-expert-portal/project-management.html#section_452032556

Milestones: You can create, delete, and add custom Milestones in a project and use them to assign due dates to Tasks

entry.title: Re: Not able to purchase one attempt for SAP certificatio...
entry.content: @Vinay34: Did you explore the backend booking ?
